speeches.name: John C. Williams
speeches.role: President and CEO, New York Fed
speeches.summary: John C. Williams stated that the current monetary policy stance is well positioned to balance risks to maximum employment and price stability, supporting the FOMC's decision to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.5-3.75%.[3]

speeches.name: Michael S. Barr
speeches.summary: Governor Michael S. Barr spoke on rural economic development at a forum on strengthening America's economy through rural investment.[8]

speeches.name: Alberto Musalem
speeches.summary: St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem supported keeping the policy rate at 3.5-3.75%, viewing it as appropriately balanced amid risks of persistent inflation due to geopolitical factors and tariffs, expecting it to remain suitable for some time.[1]

speeches.name: Michael S. Barr
speeches.summary: Governor Michael S. Barr supported the FOMC's decision to maintain current monetary policy, highlighting economic resilience amid shocks and the need for vigilance against inflation persistence.[2]

speeches.name: Susan M. Collins
speeches.summary: Boston Fed President Susan M. Collins delivered observations on the economic outlook and small businesses at the Outlook 2026 event.[6]

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Hawkish: CPI at 3.3%, Fed Funds at 3.64%, unemployment at 4.3%.
